growth_picks.source_detail: Found by BOTH independent discovery AND scanner
catalysts_general: Discovery: Micron entered high-volume HBM4 production for NVIDIA Vera Rubin on March 17 and followed with record fiscal Q2 results on March 18, making it one of the clearest public beneficiaries of the still-tight HBM and AI-memory cycle.
growth_picks.analyst_take: AI-memory demand is still outrunning supply, and Micron's record March results, HBM4 ramp, and meaningful director buying support further upside despite the usual memory-cycle risk.
High-margin, cash-generative software franchise trading about 47% below its 52-week high, with buyback support and AI product expansion outweighing near-term tax-season and Mailchimp execution risk.

catalysts_general: Discovery: Broadcom remains one of the highest-conviction AI infrastructure beneficiaries after posting record March-quarter results, triple-digit AI revenue growth, and much stronger-than-expected forward guidance.
growth_picks.analyst_take: Broadcom still looks like a high-quality AI infrastructure winner after its March 4, 2026 Q1 release showed 29% revenue growth, 106% AI revenue growth to $8.4B, and Q2 guidance for $22.0B revenue with AI semiconductor revenue rising to $10.7B.

catalysts_general: Discovery: Constellation remains one of the cleanest public ways to express institutional rotation into AI-power and nuclear-backed electricity scarcity.
growth_picks.analyst_take: Constellation is a credible AI-power and nuclear scarcity winner with newly raised 2026 earnings power, long-dated contracted load growth, and visible Calpine synergies, though the stock is too expensive for a stronger call.

growth_picks.source_detail: Found by BOTH independent discovery AND scanner
catalysts_general: Discovery: ServiceNow is one of the few enterprise software names with visible AI monetization, as January results showed Now Assist net new ACV more than doubled year over year and RPO climbed to $28.2 billion, while recent customer rollouts such as Fiserv show AI moving deeper into regulated workflows.
growth_picks.analyst_take: ServiceNow combines rare large-cap software quality with visible AI monetization, strong backlog growth, and fresh insider buying after a 50% drawdown, but it is still not cheap enough for a stronger call.
NVIDIA is still the highest-quality public AI infrastructure franchise, with record FY2026 results and fresh Rubin-era demand signals offsetting regulatory and capacity risks.

Gen Digital is a high-margin cash compounder trading like a no-growth levered roll-up even after 26% Q3 revenue growth, raised FY26 guidance, and a March 2026 debt extension that reduced near-term refinancing pressure.
Deeply discounted, cash-generative gaming hardware name with a new $50M buyback and Fanatec/peripherals momentum, though cyclical component weakness keeps this out of strong-buy territory.
Cheap, cash-generative payments franchise with buyback support and a clearer post-Worldpay setup, but the integration and leverage burden keep this from a high-conviction call.
Domino's offers resilient franchise cash generation and a near-term earnings catalyst, but elevated leverage and ongoing margin pressure cap conviction.

catalysts_general: Discovery: Oracle's March 10 quarter proved AI demand is converting into financials, with cloud revenue up 44%, IaaS up 84%, multicloud database revenue up 531%, and RPO up 325%, while March 17 NVIDIA announcements showed OCI still widening its technical edge for large-scale training and inference.
growth_picks.analyst_take: Oracle's March quarter confirmed AI cloud demand is converting into revenue and backlog, and the stock still prices in too much financing fear after a 57% drawdown from its 52-week high.

growth_picks.source_detail: Found by BOTH independent discovery AND scanner
catalysts_general: Discovery: Jeff Green's roughly $148 million open-market purchase after the post-earnings collapse is one of the clearest insider-conviction signals in large-cap software this quarter.
growth_picks.analyst_take: TTD looks like a contrarian buy after a 75% drawdown because Tier 1-2 sources still show durable retention, positive cash generation, and unusually strong insider conviction, but the setup depends on management proving the Q1 slowdown is temporary.

catalysts_general: Discovery: Arista's March 12 XPO optics announcement points to another leg of AI-cluster scaling, where liquid-cooled optical interconnect density becomes a hard constraint and Arista keeps monetizing the network layer around AI factories.
growth_picks.analyst_take: High-quality AI networking compounder with strong margins and balance sheet strength, but still priced for execution so upside depends on another clean AI Ethernet ramp.

catalysts_general: Discovery: Roper has been hit with a textbook capitulation unwind, falling 40.65% from its 52-week high and only 12.42% above its low even though it still earns $1.54 billion with 28.29% operating margins; the catalyst is the next Q1 earnings check later in April 2026 after a cluster of January downgrades at the lows, but the bear case is that organic growth has structurally slowed and the market is right to rerate the acquisitive model.
growth_picks.analyst_take: High-margin vertical software compounder now trades at a much more reasonable valuation after a 40% drawdown, and late-April earnings are the next key test of whether the growth slowdown is temporary rather than structural.

catalysts_general: Discovery: International Paper is messy but washed out, sitting 37.64% below its 52-week high and just 4.26% above its low with a still-liquid $18.53 billion market cap; the catalyst stack is CEO Andrew Silvernail's January 30, 2026 open-market buy, the planned split into two public companies announced January 29, and first-quarter earnings due April 30, 2026, while the bear case is that the impairment-heavy 2025 print reflects deeper integration and pricing problems than the market is assuming.
growth_picks.analyst_take: International Paper is a beaten-down packaging turnaround with credible insider alignment, a near-term earnings catalyst on April 30, 2026, and a restructuring/split path that could unlock value if North American pricing and cost-out execution hold.

Cheap, cash-generative toy and IP owner with a fresh CEO buy, active buyback, and 2026 entertainment and digital catalysts, though margin pressure and softer core brands keep it out of high-conviction territory.

Adobe is a high-margin, cash-rich software franchise trading 42.5% below its 52-week high, with Q1 FY2026 execution still solid, but the stock needs proof that Firefly and Acrobat AI monetization can outrun CEO-transition and competitive fears.

Workday looks like a reset-quality software name with strong free cash flow and a cheaper forward multiple, but the upside depends on Aneel Bhusri's AI push translating into durable bookings growth rather than just narrative.

growth_picks.source_detail: Found by BOTH independent discovery AND scanner
catalysts_general: Discovery: Lululemon is a hated former premium-growth darling now down 55.00% from its 52-week high and only 6.35% off the low while still earning $1.58 billion with 20.12% operating margins; the catalyst is the post-crash reset into the June 4, 2026 earnings report after the March 17 guide-down, but the bear case is that U.S. demand softness and an unresolved leadership transition turn this into a value trap.
growth_picks.analyst_take: Cheap for a still-profitable premium brand, but the March guide-down, weak North America trends, and unresolved CEO transition keep the turnaround unproven.

GLP offers durable fuel-distribution cash flow and a still-growing payout, but limited upside, recent insider selling, and borrowing-heavy balance-sheet risk keep it in income-hold territory rather than a fresh buy.

growth_picks.source_detail: Found by BOTH independent discovery AND scanner
catalysts_general: Discovery: Replimune is a classic FDA-event setup with an April 10, 2026 action date for RP1 where approval could quickly re-rate a small-cap oncology story.
growth_picks.analyst_take: April 10 FDA action on RP1 creates real upside, but after the 2025 CRL this is still a binary regulatory trade with insufficient edge for a fresh buy.

catalysts_general: Discovery: Cisco has crossed from AI narrative to AI revenue, with February Q2 results showing $2.1 billion of hyperscaler AI infrastructure orders and March launches extending Secure AI Factory with NVIDIA plus 102.4 Tbps Silicon One switching into edge and enterprise deployments.
growth_picks.analyst_take: Cisco has real AI and networking demand plus strong cash generation, but the stock is only about 12% below its 52-week high and looks closer to fairly valued than mispriced.

catalysts_general: Discovery: Pentagon framework agreements to surge missile output and Europe's accelerated rearmament should support incremental demand for Lockheed's precision fires, PAC-3, and long-range strike portfolio.
growth_picks.analyst_take: Record backlog and missile-demand acceleration support durable cash flow, but modest upside and unresolved F-35/classified-program execution risk make Lockheed Martin more of a hold than a fresh buy here.

catalysts_general: Discovery: The new US-Japan strategic investment package explicitly backs up to $40 billion of GE Vernova Hitachi SMR deployment in Tennessee and Alabama, turning alliance politics into a named order pipeline.
growth_picks.analyst_take: GE Vernova has credible grid, gas, and nuclear demand catalysts, but with the stock near highs and consensus upside only modest, the setup no longer offers attractive value-adjusted entry.

catalysts_general: Discovery: BORR logged a two-director buying cluster on March 24, 2026, with Tor Olav Troim buying about $2.60 million and Jeffrey Currie buying about $1.33 million, a $3.93 million combined vote of confidence in a beaten-down offshore driller.
growth_picks.analyst_take: Insider buying and improving jack-up contract coverage support the story, but BORR is already near its 52-week high and leverage plus Gulf/Mexico execution risk make the risk-reward only balanced here.

catalysts_general: Discovery: President and CEO Richard Young bought 1.0M shares for $1.40M on March 25, 2026 while EVP and General Counsel David Savarie and director John Seaman added another $54.5K by March 30, a three-person cluster after a roughly 25.9% monthly decline.
growth_picks.analyst_take: Fresh March recapitalization and CEO-led insider buying improved IAUX's survival odds, but it is still a high-burn, no-reserve Nevada buildout story that must prove execution before it deserves a BUY.

catalysts_general: Discovery: United Therapeutics printed a fresh biotech catalyst with late-March TETON-1 success, adding meaningful upside optionality beyond its already cash-generative pulmonary franchise.
growth_picks.analyst_take: United Therapeutics has real upside optionality from March's TETON-1 and ralinepag wins plus a fresh $2 billion buyback, but with shares near 52-week highs and recent insider flow skewing to 10b5-1 sales/exercises, the risk/reward is no longer attractive enough for a fresh buy.

catalysts_general: Discovery: Rockwell is a credible physical-AI second derivative because first-quarter 2026 segment sales rebounded sharply and management tied wins to AI-enabled industrial troubleshooting, while its OTTO autonomous mobile robot production ramp in Milwaukee gives it tangible exposure to warehouse and factory automation.
growth_picks.analyst_take: Rockwell has a credible automation and industrial-AI recovery story, but the rebound is already carrying a premium valuation while services softness and capex uncertainty limit near-term upside.

catalysts_general: Discovery: HUM is about 45% off its 52-week high and only around 6% above its low, so the April 6, 2026 CMS rate finalization plus April 29, 2026 earnings create a clear reset window, but Humana stays highly exposed if Medicare Advantage medical-cost pressure does not ease.
growth_picks.analyst_take: Humana is statistically cheap and has a live April policy/earnings reset, but Medicare Advantage rate pressure and the company's sharply reduced 2026 earnings base keep the stock in prove-it mode.

catalysts_general: Discovery: PAYC is about 55% below its 52-week high and only around 16% above its low, so May 6, 2026 earnings are the next chance for management to clear a low bar while a still-live $1.5 billion buyback authorization supports downside, though self-service automation can keep cannibalizing growth if execution slips.
growth_picks.analyst_take: PAYC is a profitable HCM software franchise trading at a compressed multiple with buyback support, but 2026 guidance slowed to 6%-7% revenue growth and recent insider filings show grants plus tax-withholding sales rather than open-market buying.

catalysts_general: Discovery: Vail is still a hated consumer-discretionary cyclical, but a $4.9 million CEO purchase after a multiyear slide and poor snow season makes the setup meaningfully more interesting.
growth_picks.analyst_take: CEO Robert Katz's $4.9 million open-market buy and still-positive pass sales argue the stock is stabilizing, but weather-hit traffic, high leverage, and fresh litigation keep the setup short of a buy.

catalysts_general: Discovery: Broad Section 232 steel tariffs and the removal of exemptions improve domestic price realization for Nucor's flat-roll and plate businesses while import competition is constrained.
growth_picks.analyst_take: Tariff-backed domestic pricing and management's stronger Q1 outlook support an earnings rebound, but limited upside from current levels and cyclical cash-flow risk keep Nucor below a clean buy threshold.

growth_picks.source_detail: Found by BOTH independent discovery AND scanner
catalysts_general: Discovery: PYPL sits roughly 43% below its 52-week high and only about 18% above its low, with Apr. 28, 2026 earnings and the March 17, 2026 expansion of PYUSD to 70 markets as concrete near-term catalysts, though checkout growth and stablecoin monetization still need to prove they are big enough to matter.
growth_picks.analyst_take: PayPal is cheap and cash-generative, but the turnaround still needs proof because branded checkout slowed sharply and the new CEO has only just taken over.

catalysts_general: Discovery: Sysco became a contrarian idea after the market punished its Restaurant Depot deal even though the transaction gives it immediate scale in a faster-growing cash-and-carry channel.
growth_picks.analyst_take: Core operations improved into fiscal Q2, but the debt-heavy Restaurant Depot deal adds enough leverage, dilution, and regulatory risk to keep Sysco in wait-and-see territory despite the selloff.

catalysts_general: Discovery: Grocery Outlet has seen repeated open-market purchases by the CEO and multiple directors near 52-week lows, which is exactly the kind of cluster buying that can mark a washout bottom.
growth_picks.analyst_take: Insider cluster buying and heavy short interest create rebound optionality, but management's own 2026 guide and 36-store optimization plan show Grocery Outlet is still in a messy turnaround rather than a clean value buy.

catalysts_general: Discovery: Ziff Davis looks like an avoid candidate because after agreeing to sell its stable Connectivity unit, the remaining portfolio is more exposed to AI-answer and zero-click pressure in media categories such as health, shopping, and reviews.
growth_picks.analyst_take: The $1.2 billion Connectivity sale and a fresh activist stake create real optionality, but the remaining digital-media portfolio still faces explicit AI and search-traffic pressure and management has not yet framed the post-sale earnings power clearly enough for a buy.
High-margin HCM compounder with strong cash flow and shareholder returns, but Paycor-driven growth, higher debt, and limited upside keep the risk/reward only average.

High-quality Permian operator with real buyback and pipeline catalysts, but at 3.5% below its 52-week high and only about 7% below the analyst target, the risk/reward is not attractive enough for a fresh contrarian buy.
Gartner looks optically cheap and highly cash generative after a 65% drawdown, but slowing contract-value growth, weaker retention, and no meaningful insider buying keep the setup in prove-it mode.

Concentrix looks optically cheap after the goodwill-writeoff selloff, but recent filings still show margin compression, negative Q1 adjusted free cash flow, and leverage/rating pressure that need to improve before the stock earns a buy.
Coty is optically cheap and still throws off cash, but withdrawn FY26 guidance, weak Consumer Beauty trends, and uncertain asset-sale value make the turnaround unproven.
Snap is cheap and cash-generative enough to stay on the watchlist, but soft near-term ad guidance and rising child-safety scrutiny keep it in prove-it mode.

catalysts_general: Discovery: The end of Russian gas transit through Ukraine and EU restrictions on Russian LNG transshipment make Venture Global's rapidly ramping Plaquemines volumes strategically more valuable to Europe.
growth_picks.analyst_take: Venture Global is executing on Plaquemines and CP2, but the stock still looks too expensive for a business carrying extreme leverage, deeply negative free cash flow, and unresolved Calcasieu arbitration exposure.

catalysts_general: Discovery: ULCC screens as a live squeeze candidate with 42.4% short interest and 6.0 days to cover on Benzinga's March 17, 2026 table, while Frontier's March 20 8-K raised Q1 revenue guidance to up 5% to 9% and projected more than $900 million of quarter-end liquidity.
growth_picks.analyst_take: Revenue trends improved into spring, but ULCC still looks like a heavily levered turnaround with fuel and execution risk high enough that the short-squeeze setup is not a sound long thesis.
